Home / Alternatives to InVision / InVision vs Excalidraw

InVision vs Excalidraw

A side-by-side look at InVision (the paid SaaS) and Excalidraw (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

InVision Excalidraw
Tagline Design collaboration, prototyping and whiteboards. Virtual whiteboard for sketchy diagrams.
License Proprietary SaaS MIT
Pricing Freehand Basic free; Pro from $4.95/user/month (limited availability 2024+). Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 1/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Windows, macOS, Linux
Mobile apps Official apps typically available iOS, Android
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Quick wireframes when you don't need high-fidelity prototypes.

Excalidraw strengths

  • Great for wireframes and brainstorms.
  • End-to-end encrypted rooms on official instance.
  • Embeddable in docs and wikis.

Excalidraw weaknesses

  • Not a full design tool — sketch-style only.
  • Limited component/library system.
  • Real-time plus is behind a paid tier.

What's the catch with InVision?

  • InVision shuttered its core prototyping product in late 2024.
  • Users are actively searching for replacements.
  • Data export and migration were messy.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to InVision: see InVision alternatives, or learn more about Excalidraw on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
